name: Signal Event Rate Heuristic Registry
summary: Family-local interpretation of shared heuristics for waveform-to-event-to-rate pipelines.

family_notes: Waveform steps can erase context before the detector sees the raw boundary.
Cleanup before filtering is preferred when the boundary itself is unstable.
admissibility_notes: Prefer cleanup before the first major transform when abrupt input steps are present.
escalation_conditions: Escalate when discontinuities occur before the first conditioning stage.
uncertainty_notes: Use as a pre-filter warning, not as a universal rejection signal.

references.title: Signal event rate heuristic registry
references.note: Local family interpretation of the shared boundary heuristic.

family_notes: A local quality gate helps avoid committing to one brittle conditioning path.
Branching is justified when the waveform quality changes materially over time.
admissibility_notes: Quality variation should not silently force one brittle path when a gate or alternate branch is available.
escalation_conditions: Escalate when local quality changes enough that one fixed path is likely to fail on part of the window.
uncertainty_notes: Windowed quality metrics are heuristic and should not be treated as a universal failure signal.

references.title: Signal event rate heuristic registry
references.note: Local family interpretation of the shared quality heuristic.

family_notes: The detector output should not drift into implausible event spacing before rate estimation.
After detection, timing cleanup is preferable to letting the rate estimator absorb the error.
admissibility_notes: Downstream measure stages should not trust unstable event spacing without cleanup or smoothing.
escalation_conditions: Escalate when implausible event spacing persists after an otherwise valid detector path.
uncertainty_notes: This is an after-detection heuristic only.

references.title: Signal event rate heuristic registry
references.note: Local family interpretation of the shared interval heuristic.

family_notes: Detected events can drift away from the conditioned waveform or the downstream rate expectation.
Alignment errors often show up as a structural mismatch before they show up as a score drop.
admissibility_notes: Treat detector-to-measure misalignment as a structural warning before retuning parameters.
escalation_conditions: Escalate when outputs no longer line up with the conditioned representation they were derived from.
uncertainty_notes: Use when the detector or post-detection stage appears structurally misaligned.

references.title: Signal event rate heuristic registry
references.note: Local family interpretation of the shared alignment heuristic.

family_notes: The pipeline should not lose the waveform boundary before the detector can use it.
If the required inputs or adjacency assumptions are absent, treat the candidate as risky.
admissibility_notes: Boundary and adjacency assumptions should remain explicit before downstream conversion becomes lossy.
escalation_conditions: Escalate when required boundary inputs or stage adjacency assumptions are absent.
uncertainty_notes: Useful as a general admissibility heuristic for signal-style pipelines.

references.title: Signal event rate heuristic registry
references.note: Local family interpretation of a shared admissibility heuristic.

audit.rationale: Captures family-local readings of shared heuristics without redefining the canonical meaning.
audit.dejargonized_summary: These are the warning signs that matter most for waveform-to-event-to-rate pipelines and the structural responses they support.
uncertainty_notes: Family guidance should remain local and should not redefine the shared heuristic identifiers.

references.title: Signal event rate heuristic registry
references.note: Local transitional family registry for shared heuristic interpretation.
